jokes.text: What's that? - It's a cup. What, a new cup? Yeah, I got a new cup. Do you like it? - We've got a cup. - Yeah, we've got one cup. We needed two cups.
jokes.setup: Normal conversation about a new purchase
jokes.punchline: Reveals they previously owned only one cup between two people
jokes.explanation: Perfectly sets up the characters' poverty while being absurdly matter-of-fact about sharing one cup

jokes.text: Bret, do you even pay any attention to the cup roster? - Your cup roster.
jokes.setup: Reference to managing their single cup
jokes.punchline: They have an actual scheduled 'cup roster' for one cup
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - not only do they share one cup, they have a formal schedule for it. Perfect deadpan absurdity.

jokes.text: That means I can't drink a cup of tea between 7:00 and 9:00 pm. Well, I have it till 9:00 and then you get it for two hours. Then I use it from 11:00 till 1:00. You use it from 1:00 till 3:00.
jokes.punchline: Detailed breakdown of their ridiculous hourly cup allocation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that makes the absurdity concrete with specific times

jokes.text: And then we give the cup a rest.
jokes.punchline: Even the inanimate cup gets scheduled downtime
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper - treating an inanimate object like it needs rest time is brilliant absurdist logic

jokes.text: Okay, well, I just need a second to calm down.
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's over-the-top reaction to a tiny purchase
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their financial desperation through overreaction

jokes.text: How much was that cup again? $2.79. Oh, that's quite interesting, isn't it?
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's understated reaction to the obvious connection
jokes.explanation: Nice setup/payoff with Jemaine's dry delivery of the obvious connection

jokes.text: Yes, but the point is because of your $2.79 spending spree, we now owe $60 and our phone and gas are gonna be cut off.
jokes.punchline: Calling $2.79 a 'spending spree' with catastrophic consequences
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how their poverty amplifies small mistakes

jokes.text: Stink.
jokes.setup: Learning they owe $60 and utilities will be cut off
jokes.punchline: Bret's massively understated response to financial crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bret's casual understatement to crisis

jokes.text: Ah. We got a letter from the electricity company. What did it say? Didn't read it.
jokes.setup: Getting an official letter from utility company
jokes.punchline: Didn't bother reading what's obviously a shutoff notice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their avoidance of bad news

jokes.text: We got a letter from the electricity company. What did it say? Didn't read it.
jokes.setup: Letter from electricity company (obviously bad news)
jokes.punchline: Bret didn't even bother reading it
jokes.explanation: Classic avoidance comedy - why read bad news when you can just ignore it?

jokes.text: The bass. It's called a bass. Well, I call it the dad guitar, 'cause it's more like a... 'I'm your dad. Hey, Murray, get into the shed and get the mower... do the lawn.'
jokes.setup: Correcting that it's called a bass
jokes.punchline: Bret's weird logic about bass being like a dad voice with random impersonation
jokes.explanation: Weird Bret logic with unexpected voice work

jokes.text: You need Bret's mum guitar to add the beautiful tones. 'Come on, now. Murray's okay. He's with me. Why you get home so late, Gordon?' 'I was having a few beers.'
jokes.punchline: Now guitar is 'mum guitar' with even more elaborate domestic scene
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the guitar personification concept

jokes.text: You'd have to be deaf to hear that.
jokes.setup: Can barely hear Jemaine's bass alone
jokes.punchline: Backwards logic - being deaf would help you hear it
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that actually makes weird sense in context

jokes.text: Conchords crash and burn. Doesn't sound too good. By Murray hewitt. - You wrote this?
jokes.punchline: Reveal that their manager wrote the bad review
jokes.explanation: Great reveal that subverts expectations - their own manager is sabotaging them

jokes.text: Two stars. - Two stars? - Two stars. - You can't give us two stars, Murray. Two out of five stars? Well, I wish it was, but it's out of 100.
jokes.setup: Two star rating sounds bad but not terrible
jokes.punchline: It's two out of 100, not 5
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that makes the rating much worse than expected

jokes.text: Don't tell people that. Say it's out of five.
jokes.punchline: Murray's solution is to just lie about the scale
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray logic - instead of fixing the problem, just lie about it

jokes.text: It's a friend of mine from nigeria. - Nigel soladu. - When did you go to nigeria? Well, I didn't go. That's the best bit.
jokes.setup: Murray has invested band funds with someone from Nigeria
jokes.punchline: He thinks not meeting them in person is an advantage
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the Nigerian prince scam with Murray's naive enthusiasm

jokes.text: Why would someone want to scam me, Jemaine, and on the internet service, one of the trusted things of today's society?
jokes.punchline: Calling the internet 'one of the trusted things' while being scammed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray naivety about internet security in the mid-2000s

jokes.text: Just fill out this. Put how many biscuits you want, your name, and you'll have to bring in some I.D. That gets sent back to wellington. We'll have your biscuits by Tuesday.
jokes.punchline: Incredibly bureaucratic process requiring ID and international processing for biscuits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of bureaucratic absurdity for the simplest request

jokes.text: Mainly people who need to drink from far away.
jokes.setup: Who would want to buy superstraws?
jokes.punchline: Bret's matter-of-fact explanation for an absurd product
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bret deadpan logic for a ridiculous product

jokes.text: Wow, that's so romantic... two struggling musicians living in poverty and despair, selling their instruments.
jokes.setup: Learning they sold instruments for money
jokes.punchline: Mel romanticizes their actual suffering
jokes.explanation: Good Mel character moment - she fetishizes their poverty

jokes.text: I know it sounds good, but it's actually pretty dressing.
jokes.setup: Responding to Mel's romantic view of their poverty
jokes.punchline: Says 'dressing' instead of 'depressing'
jokes.explanation: Typical Bret malapropism that still kind of works in context

jokes.text: - Bret does. - No. - I don't. You do. - Bret does. - I don't, but Bret does. - He does. - Jemaine's the one for that. - Bret always does that.
jokes.setup: Mel asking if they do massage
jokes.punchline: Both desperately trying to volunteer the other for prostitution
jokes.explanation: Good escalating desperation as both try to avoid sex work

jokes.text: I'm just doing it very gently to begin with. - Are you... I can't really feel it. I wanna feel it.
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo in what should be therapeutic massage
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo humor, elevated slightly by Bret's awkwardness

jokes.text: Should I lay down? No no, this is good. Maybe I should take off some clothes... no no, that's good.
jokes.punchline: Awkward negotiation about massage boundaries
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe setup for the massage sequence

jokes.text: You're like a samurai, like a massage samurai.
jokes.punchline: Comparing massage to samurai warrior skills
jokes.explanation: Mel's typical over-the-top enthusiasm for mundane things

jokes.text: I'm just patting her.
jokes.setup: Mel's elaborate praise for the massage
jokes.punchline: Bret reveals he's just patting her, not massaging
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the moment - all that praise for literally just patting

jokes.text: Oh, I feel like I've hired a gigolo. I'm just kidding. I would never hire... I mean, I would, I would, but I wouldn't pay you. I would. I would pay you so much money. I'm joking. I'm not. I am. I'm not.
jokes.punchline: Mel's stream-of-consciousness contradictory thoughts about gigolos
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Mel's flustered contradictory thoughts, though a bit verbose

jokes.text: I would. I would pay you so much money. I'm just kidding. That's not how I am. That's not what I'm about. I'm joking. I'm not. I am. I'm not.
jokes.punchline: Mel's stream-of-consciousness contradicting herself repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mel's inability to control her mouth

jokes.text: Well, it's a story about a prostitute called Richard gere who gets to go out with a "pretty woman" Julia roberts who pays him a lot of money.
jokes.setup: Explaining Pretty Woman to convince Bret prostitution isn't degrading
jokes.punchline: Gets the movie completely backwards - thinks Richard Gere is the prostitute
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misunderstanding of a famous movie that completely reverses the plot

jokes.text: 'Cause we never get sex or get paid.
jokes.setup: Jemaine's optimism about getting paid for sex
jokes.punchline: Bret's matter-of-fact assessment of their romantic failures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bret logic - crushes Jemaine's fantasy with brutal honesty

jokes.text: Bret, the ladies go crazy for my sugarlumps. - Your what?
jokes.punchline: Introduces absurd euphemism 'sugarlumps' for his anatomy
jokes.explanation: Sets up the song with a perfectly ridiculous euphemism

jokes.text: ¶ let me tell you, I see you girls checking out my trunks ¶
jokes.punchline: 'Trunks' (swimwear) as euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the song with silly euphemism

jokes.text: ¶ my dungarees make them hunger-ees ¶
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'hunger-ees' to rhyme with dungarees
jokes.explanation: 'Hunger-ees' is a delightfully stupid made-up word to force the rhyme

jokes.text: ¶ they're over the moon when I don pantaloons ¶
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of Jemaine in pantaloons being sexy
jokes.explanation: Pantaloons are inherently unsexy, making the claim absurd

jokes.text: ¶ my cannonballs cause a kerfuffle ¶
jokes.punchline: 'Cannonballs' causing a 'kerfuffle'
jokes.explanation: 'Kerfuffle' is a wonderfully polite word for the chaos his testicles supposedly cause

jokes.text: ♪ when in walks a guy with his dick in a sling ♪
jokes.punchline: Injured man selling his genitals
jokes.explanation: Dark turn in the song with medical injury humor

jokes.text: ¶ I said, '10 bucks,' he said, 'no way' '10 bucks and a frisbee,' he said, 'okay' ¶
jokes.punchline: A frisbee tips the scales in this bizarre transaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - the frisbee as deal-maker in this absurd negotiation

jokes.text: ¶ and sold 'em as hacky Sacks later that day ¶
jokes.punchline: Resold them as hacky sacks
jokes.explanation: Dark and absurd - selling human testicles as toys is brilliantly wrong

jokes.text: You should be the prostitute. You bought the cup.
jokes.setup: Deciding who should be the prostitute
jokes.punchline: The cup purchase makes Bret responsible for prostitution
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - the cup purchase now directly leads to prostitution

jokes.text: Nut loaf.
jokes.punchline: Jemaine offers nut loaf as additional incentive
jokes.explanation: Perfect random non-sequitur delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: And also, I think you're gonna have to go the whole way. You can't just hug them. Just hugs until I get more confident.
jokes.punchline: Bret wants to ease into prostitution with just hugging
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bret character moment - approaching prostitution like learning to swim

jokes.text: 20-40-60. Okay, 10-30-50. $5. He'll go all the way and he's clean.
jokes.punchline: Rapidly dropping prices and overselling Bret's services
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing their desperation and poor sales skills

jokes.text: Sad because we don't have any electricity, but satisfied 'cause I was right about the cup.
jokes.setup: Bret asking what expression is on Jemaine's face
jokes.punchline: Jemaine finds satisfaction in being right despite their suffering
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Jemaine prioritizes being right over their wellbeing

jokes.text: How many straws does it take to make a superstraw? Five. What's the total cost of those five straws? Two-fift... oh.
jokes.punchline: Bret realizes he's losing money on every sale
jokes.explanation: Good setup showing their complete lack of business sense

jokes.text: Flight of the no-chords. It was hard to tell if the band was a band or a mime troop. No stars.
jokes.punchline: Even worse pun name and comparing them to mimes, zero stars
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Murray's reviews are getting worse and more hostile

jokes.text: The Mormon tabernacle choir. Boyz ii men. Bobby mcferrin. Pavarotti.
jokes.setup: Murray claiming no successful musicians without instruments
jokes.punchline: Bret lists multiple examples, proving Murray wrong
jokes.explanation: Good moment where Bret is actually right for once

jokes.text: Basically, you should have your guitars back in about 14 years.
jokes.setup: Murray's timeline to get instruments back
jokes.punchline: Impossibly long timeframe for a simple goal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how hopeless their financial situation is

jokes.text: No commission on Bret's job.
jokes.setup: Murray trying to get commission on existing job
jokes.punchline: Makes note when told he can't take commission on job Bret already has
jokes.explanation: Good Murray character moment - his inappropriate business practices

jokes.text: I was just calling to say... do you remember how we slept together about six times I think it was last year in february? Uh, yeah. I'm a prostitute now.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions becoming prostitute and wants retroactive payment
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - retroactively trying to charge for past consensual sex

jokes.text: Also, would you be interested in going out sometime?
jokes.setup: Just asked for money for past sex
jokes.punchline: Immediately transitions to asking her on a date
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - completely oblivious to how inappropriate this is

jokes.text: Just a normal book... nondescript. Just a book I had. - What book? - Just a super... a book that tells you where to sell your body.
jokes.setup: Mel claiming she read prostitution advice in a 'normal book'
jokes.punchline: Her attempts to sound casual about owning prostitution guide
jokes.explanation: Good Mel character moment - her obvious lies about the prostitution book

jokes.text: Can you imagine it? Out of the hundreds of people that use the internet, only I was willing to help him.
jokes.punchline: Thinks hundreds (not millions) use internet, and he's specially chosen
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray naivety - massively underestimates internet users and feels special

jokes.text: Why aren't I a prostitute? Good one, nigel. Why isn't nigel a prostitute? We could all be in a prostitute club.
jokes.setup: Discussion about who should be a prostitute
jokes.punchline: Murray's enthusiasm for group prostitution club
jokes.explanation: Great Murray escalation - turns prostitution into a social club

jokes.text: Nagoota tata lacie.
jokes.setup: Nigel saying something in his language
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up gibberish presented as foreign language
jokes.explanation: Standard fake foreign language gag

jokes.text: There's the keyboard here. There's the mouse. You can just go up and down on the keys. I don't use that. There's the TV screen.
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of how computers work
jokes.explanation: Good Murray tech ignorance - treating computer like a confusing appliance

jokes.text: ¶ you can say no to being a night-look, a boy hook, a red boy bro-ho ¶
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous made-up terms like 'bro-ho'
jokes.explanation: 'Bro-ho' and 'red boy' are silly made-up terms that sound plausible

jokes.text: ¶ do you have any other skills... like typing? ¶
jokes.setup: Suggesting Jemaine has other skills besides prostitution
jokes.punchline: 'Typing' as the hopeful alternative career
jokes.explanation: Sweet optimism - typing as the practical alternative to sex work

jokes.text: I have a few rules. No laughing, especially during, okay? It puts me off.
jokes.punchline: Setting professional boundaries about not laughing during sex
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jemaine's sexual insecurity even as prostitute

jokes.text: No no, I'm just the guy that wears the big condom.
jokes.setup: Police asking if he's also a prostitute
jokes.punchline: Bret's matter-of-fact description of his costume job
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that makes the costume sound even more ridiculous

jokes.text: I knew if you bought a cup, we'd end up in jail. I knew it.
jokes.punchline: Blaming their arrest entirely on the cup purchase
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend callback - the cup directly led to jail through chain of events

jokes.text: Jemaine, was it a good thing to prostitute yourself? - Well... - no, it wasn't.
jokes.punchline: Murray answers his own question before Jemaine can respond
jokes.explanation: Good Murray character moment - asking questions just to make his own points

jokes.text: We were at square two, but now we're back. - When was that? - Let's stop talking about squares.
jokes.setup: Murray explaining they're back at square one
jokes.punchline: Can't explain when they were at square two, avoids the topic
jokes.explanation: Good Murray deflection when caught in nonsensical metaphor

jokes.text: You're like a couple of... jailhouse turkeys.
jokes.punchline: Makes up nonsensical insult 'jailhouse turkeys'
jokes.explanation: Classic Murray attempt at tough talk that makes no sense
